title: audit — published events
description: 6 published events covering the audit-row insert lifecycle (AuditRecorded), retention-policy CRUD, retention-override expiry, purge execution, and export request. All events dispatched afterCommit so a rolled-back transaction does not leak signals to listeners. AuditRecorded is DELIBERATELY narrow — it carries only the audit_id + minimal metadata so downstream listeners must re-read the audit row if they need diff details (prevents leaking PII into event queues).

events.name: AuditRecorded
events.trigger: AuditObserver.created — vendor `AuditableObserver` wrote an `audits` row, AuditObserver validated it, event fires afterCommit. Subscribed for downstream compliance signals ONLY; NEVER used to mutate audit data. Payload is intentionally narrow — no old_values/new_values, no field-level diff details — so consumers who need those details must query the audit row via AuditRepository. This prevents PII from leaking into the event queue's serialized payloads.
consumers: monitoring::EmitAuditFireRateListener — feeds an ops signal for detecting unusual audit-fire spikes (potential data-mutation storm)

activity::CorrelateAuditToActivityListener (planned Wave 6) — some domain mutations correlate an audit row to an activity_log row; the listener adds `related_audit_id` on the corresponding activity row

payload.event: string (created / updated / deleted / restored / custom event)

events.name: AuditRetentionPolicyCreated
events.trigger: AuditRetentionPolicyObserver.created — a new tenant-scoped retention override was persisted.
consumers: audit::DispatchRetentionPolicyExtendedNotificationListener — fires AuditRetentionPolicyExtendedNotification to the tenant's compliance officer
audit::InvalidateRetentionCacheListener — invalidates the per-tenant retention cache so PurgeExpiredAuditsJob picks up the new cutoff on its next run

events.name: AuditRetentionPolicyUpdated
events.trigger: AuditRetentionPolicyObserver.updated — an existing retention override was mutated (retention_years, override_ends_at, or override_reason). Rate-limited to a 1/min per-tenant cap to prevent noisy churn.
consumers: audit::InvalidateRetentionCacheListener — same as above; the cache eviction is unconditional on Created OR Updated

events.name: AuditRetentionExtensionExpired
events.trigger: ExpireRetentionOverridesJob detected override_ends_at <= now() on an active policy. Retention_years is RESET to config.audit.retention.default_years (7) and the policy is soft-deleted (deleted_at = override_ends_at).
consumers: audit::DispatchExtensionExpiredNotificationListener — informs the compliance officer that the extension window closed and the tenant is back on the default 7y retention

events.name: AuditPurgeExecuted
events.trigger: PurgeExpiredAuditsJob completed a per-tenant purge cycle. Fires ONCE per tenant per nightly run, regardless of row count (fires even when row_count=0 so ops can see the job ran).
consumers: audit::WritePurgeMetaAuditListener — writes a summary audit row via the vendor Auditable trait so the trail records itself being purged (config.audit.compliance.write_purge_meta_audit=true by default)

payload.dry_run: bool (true when --dry-run flag on the console command)

events.name: AuditExportRequested
events.trigger: POST /api/v1/audits/export OR /api/v1/platform/audits/export accepted (returns 202 + audit_export_id). Fires immediately + kicks off the async export job.
consumers: audit::DispatchAuditExportJobListener — dispatches the async job that writes the jsonl.gz file to S3 + generates the signed URL
compliance::LogRegulatorExportRequestListener (Wave 6) — writes a compliance-audit row referencing the export (required by CCPA §1798.185 for regulator-initiated requests)

payload.audit_export_id: ULID (aex_) — planned but not yet allocated; short-term uses the primary key
payload.filters: jsonb (auditable_type, auditable_id, user_id, since, until — the query filters)
